titlestmt.titleproper: Life magazines featuring in utero photography by Lennart Nilsson, autographed
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 22:00

did.unittitle: Life magazines featuring in utero photography by Lennart Nilsson, autographed

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Robert Turner, RBP, FBPA, 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Life magazines featuring in utero photography by Lennart Nilsson, autographed. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes three complete issues of Life magazine with covers and stories featuring in utero photography by Lennart Nilsson (1922-2017). Nilsson was a Swedish photojournalist known for his pioneering work capturing images of human embryos in utero and extreme macro photography. All three magazines in the collection have Nilsson's autograph on the front covers. Issues include April 30, 1965, August 1990, and November 1996. The collection also includes a typewritten letter to Robert Turner verifying Nilsson signed the magazines in 1999 at the 2nd World Congress on Biomedical Communications in Amsterdam.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
